conditions: Prostate issues (misdiagnosis/incorrect problem list entry causing ongoing billing/record confusion)
medications: Naproxen 220 mg as needed for joint pain
Acetaminophen as needed (limits to recommended daily max)
health.mental_health: Feels persistent anxiety and guilt related to caregiving decisions, with frequent sleep interruption and occasional tearfulness; has not pursued formal therapy yet due to time constraints.
recent_visits: Primary care follow-up for blood pressure and medication refills (last month)
Orthopedist visit for worsening knee pain; discussed corticosteroid injection and physical therapy (2 months ago)
Physical therapy evaluation for knee strengthening and gait stability (6 weeks ago)
Gastroenterology telehealth check-in for reflux medication review (3 months ago)
Caregiver support consult with parent’s neurologist/social worker about dementia progression and safety planning (recent)

finance.debt: Carries a small remaining car loan and is using a low-interest credit card balance to cover intermittent in-home respite care and home safety purchases for her parent; worries about long-term affordability if care needs increase.
investments: High-yield savings account for emergency fund
recent_transactions: Paid $780 for two weeks of part-time in-home caregiver coverage
Purchased $165 of home safety items (door alarms, grab bars, medication lock box)
Co-pay and prescription refills totaling about $95
Monthly automatic transfer of $250 to savings
Donation of $60 to a local dementia caregiver nonprofit (in gratitude for resources)

relationships: Aging parent (lives with Chioma) with moderate dementia; Chioma is primary caregiver and healthcare proxy
Older brother (Raj Mehta) in Houston who helps financially but is less involved day-to-day, causing tension
Neighbor and informal check-in buddy (Marisol Alvarez) who can sit with her parent briefly in emergencies
Close friend from church group (Denise Carter) who provides emotional support and occasional meal drop-offs
Adult daughter (Anika Mehta) in Austin who visits monthly but has young kids and limited flexibility
social.social_media_activity: Keeps a low profile online; uses Facebook mainly for community events and caregiver support groups under a privacy-locked account, rarely posts photos due to her parent’s condition and safety concerns.
private_conversations: Discussing whether to pursue memory-care placement versus keeping her parent at home
Conflicts with her brother about division of caregiving responsibilities and medical decision-making
Concerns about her parent wandering and a recent near-miss incident
Embarrassment and fear related to the incorrect 'prostate issues' label appearing in her medical records and how to get it corrected

schedule.work_hours: Monday–Thursday 9:00am–4:30pm (hybrid); Friday 9:00am–1:00pm remote to manage caregiving and appointments
regular_appointments: Parent’s primary care visit every 6–8 weeks
Weekly pillbox setup and vitals check for parent (Sunday evenings)
Chioma’s physical therapy session once weekly (when available)
Biweekly caregiver support group (Tuesday evenings, virtual)
upcoming_events: Home health agency intake assessment for additional respite hours (next Wednesday, 10:00am)
Orthopedist follow-up to discuss knee injection (in 3 weeks)
Nonprofit quarterly grant reporting deadline and board meeting (end of month)
Parent’s dental appointment with sedation discussion (in 2 weeks)
schedule.daily_routine: Wakes around 5:30am to help her parent with hygiene, breakfast, and medications; works mid-day with frequent check-ins and door alarms active; evenings revolve around dinner, calming routines to reduce sundowning, and preparing for the next day; sleep is fragmented by her parent’s nighttime confusion.

professional.company: North Texas Community Wellness Alliance (Dallas-based nonprofit focused on food access and senior services)
projects: Coordinating a senior meal delivery partnership with two Dallas-area churches
Managing volunteer onboarding and background checks for home-visit programs
Tracking outcomes for a county-funded caregiver respite pilot
Preparing quarterly grant reports and compliance documentation
professional.performance_issues: Has needed occasional last-minute schedule changes due to her parent’s agitation and medical appointments; worries colleagues notice her reduced availability during late afternoons and has requested more remote days without disclosing all details.
workplace_relationships: Supportive supervisor (Ellen Park) who values her experience but expects timely reporting
Tension with a newer staff member who prefers in-office coordination and questions hybrid arrangements
Strong rapport with volunteer coordinator and partner church administrators who rely on her consistency

hobbies: Water aerobics at a local recreation center (when respite coverage allows)
Cooking West African and Indian fusion meals for family
Knitting simple scarves for donation drives
habits: Keeps a written log of her parent’s behaviors, sleep, and triggers to share with clinicians
Limits driving at night due to knee pain and fatigue
Uses door alarms and a discreet GPS tag for her parent’s safety (kept private from most neighbors)
Has an occasional evening glass of wine to unwind but avoids it when her parent is restless
Practices short breathing exercises during work breaks to manage caregiver stress
lifestyle.living_situation: Owns a modest single-story home in Dallas; her parent lives with her in a converted guest room. The house has added safety measures (locks, alarms, grab bars) and a designated medication cabinet kept secured.
lifestyle.dietary_preferences: Tries to follow a heart-healthy, lower-sodium diet; prioritizes vegetables, lentils, fish, and whole grains, but relies on easy-to-prepare meals on high-stress days due to limited time and fatigue.
